query: Johor state election 2026 expected outcome, green wave PN Perikatan Nasional Malay voters, Barisan Nasional incumbency, Onn Hafiz popularity
answer:
I've completed the research. The full markdown synthesis is in my response above, and the structured findings (key facts, sources, and quantitative data points) have been recorded successfully.

**Bottom line on the Johor 2026 dynamics (data, not forecast):** The incumbent BN under MB Onn Hafiz Ghazi enters the 11 July 2026 election from a position of strength — a 40/56-seat 2022 base, a favorable economic record, and a fractured opposition. Analysts widely expect BN to retain power (one projection: BN 36–42, PH 10–16, PN 2–5). The key swing variables are: (1) **low projected turnout (45–50%)**, which structurally favors BN's grassroots machine over PH's urban/youth base; (2) **PN's collapse into infighting** (PAS–Bersatu split on 8 June) plus **PAS instructing supporters to back BN** where PN isn't standing — fueling "Green Wave"/"secret pact" allegations and effectively consolidating the Malay-Muslim conservative vote behind BN rather than PN; and (3) the **~1.2 million young, ideologically-unanchored voters** who could act as kingmakers in marginal seats but have low turnout propensity. The new party Bersama is a wildcard that may split the progressive/Chinese vote to BN's benefit.

sources.title: 2026 Johor state election - Wikipedia
sources.snippet: Election expected 11 July 2026; dissolution announced by Onn Hafiz Ghazi on 1 June 2026; all 56 seats contested.

sources.title: Johor state election: BN, PH, PN clashes expected in 32 seats
sources.snippet: 32 of 56 seats see three-cornered BN-PH-PN contests; BN and PH field all 56, PN distributes 32 seats among Bersatu, PAS, MIPP.

sources.title: Johor election key contests - Machap, Puteri Wangsa, Bukit Kepong in spotlight
sources.snippet: Onn Hafiz faces straight fight in Machap; voting 11 July; contest widely seen as a referendum.

sources.title: Cheng Mengkai: Johor election 'urgent situation tactics' still work
sources.snippet: 172 candidates, 56 seats; projected 45-50% turnout; analyst projects BN 36-42, PH 10-16, PN 2-5.

sources.title: PAS Central Committee meeting; Hadi's four Johor resolutions
sources.snippet: PAS-Bersatu suspended cooperation 8 June; PN contests 33 seats (Bersatu 16, PAS 11, MIPP 5, Pejuang 1); 'Jom Jaga Johor' campaign.

sources.title: PAS urges voters to reject Pakatan, Bersatu says voters free to decide
sources.snippet: Hadi urges rejecting PH even in straight BN-PH fights; PAS to vote BN where it isn't contesting; Muhyiddin says Bersatu members free to decide.

sources.title: Mere denial of 'secret pact' with PAS not enough, BN told
sources.snippet: Analysts debate BN-PN tacit understanding; PN fields 33 vs BN/PH 56; Onn Hafiz says BN governs alone; Hadi says PAS aims to be 'adviser'.

sources.title: PAS-Bersatu split campaigns will affect voter confidence, say analysts
sources.snippet: Analysts warn PN fragmentation undermines confidence; Bersatu more damaged after Hamzah Zainudin ouster.

sources.title: PH targeting Bersatu voters in Larkin amid PN rift
sources.snippet: PN won 14,348 votes in Larkin in 2022 (~10,000 Bersatu); PH targets former Bersatu supporters.

sources.title: Johor polls: Young, fence-sitting voters may hold sway, say analysts
sources.snippet: 1.29M+ voters under 40; ~1.2M aged 18-39 most influential; young first-timers potential kingmakers.

sources.title: Johor election begins; can BN's momentum guarantee a win?
sources.snippet: BN won 40/56 in 2022; 6.4% 2024 growth, RM110bn investments, RM2.6bn revenue; 2.72M voters, ~48% aged 18-39; GE15 Johor turnout 72.66% vs 54.92% state.

sources.title: PM popular, but won't call snap polls, say experts
sources.snippet: Anwar 51% approval (Merdeka Center); analysts say PH best-placed nationally; BN wants Johor momentum.

sources.title: YOURSAY | Johor crystal ball: Stay loyal or demand change?
sources.snippet: Debate on BN landslide vs Bersama disruption; 'Johor is UMNO fortress'/'blue wave'; PAS tells members vote BN.

sources.title: YOURSAY | DAP may not face wipeout in Johor
sources.snippet: DAP contests 17 seats (held 10 in 2022); may win fewer than 10; Bersama could draw Chinese voters.

sources.title: Zhou Zhongxin: BN's real campaign axis is the race card
sources.snippet: 2022 state election: BN 40/56 at 54.9% turnout; GE15 (74.5% turnout) PH led 31 seats; combined BN+PN would have flipped some seats.

sources.title: COMMENT | Is Anwar better for non-Malays, Malaysia?
sources.snippet: PN not contesting 23 of 56 seats described as major advantage for BN; election a GE16 indicator.
